assessment_meta.title: SL Instruments — Technical Aptitude Screen
assessment_meta.intent: Screen for hands-on technical intuition in electronics, signal processing, and fabrication. No prior project knowledge required or expected. Tests instinct, not lookup.

questions.question: A piezo disc sensor has very high source impedance. What input impedance should the amplifier stage present?
questions.metadata: Fundamental electronics — piezo loading. Wrong answer means candidate will destroy signal before it reaches the ADC.

questions.question: You compute an FFT and see signal energy smearing into adjacent frequency bins. What is the standard remedy?

options.B: Apply a window function (e.g. Hann) before the FFT

questions.metadata: Spectral leakage — a builder who has run real FFTs knows this immediately.

questions.question: You bond a sensor to a surface using cyanoacrylate and leave excess adhesive pooled around the edges. The most likely consequence is:

options.C: Spectral distortion from added mass and damping

questions.metadata: Mechanical intuition. Excess adhesive is a documented failure mode — tests whether candidate has bonded things, not just read about it.

questions.question: Which item in a typical lab is most likely to radiate ultrasonic electrical noise that contaminates a sensitive measurement?
questions.metadata: Real-world lab awareness. SMPS chargers are well-known ultrasonic noise sources.

questions.question: A sensitive analog measurement shows 50 Hz hum that disappears when you disconnect one cable. The most likely root cause is:

options.A: A ground loop in the cable routing

questions.metadata: Classic analog debugging. Anyone who has run a real measurement bench has fought a ground loop.

questions.question: You need to reliably capture signal content up to 80 kHz. What is the minimum acceptable sampling rate?
questions.metadata: Nyquist — must be 2× the highest frequency of interest. Fundamental for any signal capture work.

questions.question: An FFT shows a resonance peak that is very narrow and very tall. This indicates:

options.A: High Q-factor — low damping, sharp resonance
options.B: Low Q-factor — high damping, broad response
options.C: A noise artefact from a ground loop
options.D: A hardware fault in the ADC

questions.metadata: Q-factor intuition. Narrow = high Q = low damping. Essential for interpreting resonance data.

questions.question: A multichannel audio capture pipeline occasionally drops samples mid-session. The most likely cause is:

options.B: Buffer underrun or insufficient CPU headroom

questions.metadata: Embedded/DAQ reality check. Buffer management is the most common source of dropped-sample bugs.

questions.question: You mount a resonant test object directly onto a rigid steel plate bolted to the workbench. The core problem with this setup is:

options.C: Environmental vibrations from the bench couple directly into the measurement

questions.metadata: Vibration isolation — a critical but commonly overlooked mechanical principle in precision measurement.

questions.question: You train a classifier on data collected in the morning and test it on data from the afternoon of the same session. Accuracy is 97%. What should concern you?

options.A: The model is almost certainly production-ready
options.B: The feature set is probably too large
options.C: The features are likely poorly chosen
options.D: Temporal correlation between training and test sets may have caused data leakage

questions.metadata: Data leakage awareness. Session-based splits are a classic source of inflated accuracy in low-data experiments.

questions.question: SNR improves by 20 dB. By what factor has the signal-to-noise power ratio changed?
questions.metadata: dB arithmetic. 20 dB = 10^(20/10) = 100× power ratio. Separates candidates who understand dB from those who have only seen the symbol.

questions.question: Setup A measures a resonance frequency as: 1000 Hz, 1002 Hz, 999 Hz across three runs. Setup B gives: 1000 Hz, 1150 Hz, 870 Hz. Which setup is more repeatable?

options.D: Cannot be determined from this data

questions.metadata: Direct variance comparison. Tests whether candidate understands repeatability vs single-point accuracy.

questions.question: You need to measure the natural resonant frequency of a metal object without the fixture influencing the result. The best support method is:

options.A: Epoxy the object to a heavy steel plate
options.B: Hold it by hand for each measurement
options.C: Suspend or rest it on soft elastomer isolators
options.D: Place it directly on rough concrete

questions.metadata: Fixture design — soft mounts decouple the test object from environmental vibration. Critical for clean resonance measurement.

questions.question: You have 180 labeled samples for a classification task. Which validation approach gives the most reliable performance estimate?

options.B: No validation — just report training accuracy
options.C: Train and test on the same full dataset

questions.metadata: Low-data validation fundamentals. k-fold maximises use of limited labeled data.

questions.question: A classifier that was performing at 72% accuracy suddenly jumps to 99% after a minor code change. The correct first response is:

options.B: Investigate for data leakage — the new code may have introduced label contamination
options.C: Add more training data to confirm

questions.metadata: Scientific debugging instinct. Sudden large improvements almost always mean a bug, not a breakthrough.

questions.question: Why are balanced differential signal connections preferred over single-ended for long cable runs in precision analog measurement?

options.C: They reject common-mode noise picked up along the cable

questions.metadata: CMRR — fundamental analog signal integrity. A candidate who has wired a real measurement rig knows this.

questions.question: During prototyping you need to bond a sensor temporarily — you will reposition it multiple times. The most appropriate adhesive is:

options.D: Beeswax or a reversible thermoplastic adhesive

questions.metadata: Practical adhesive knowledge. Beeswax is a standard acoustic measurement adhesive precisely because it is reversible.

questions.question: Two peaks in your FFT are very close together in frequency and are blending into one. To separate them clearly, you should:

options.B: Increase the FFT window length by capturing more samples
options.D: Reduce the number of measurement channels

questions.metadata: Frequency resolution = sampling rate / FFT size. More samples = finer resolution. Core DSP.

questions.question: You have 500 labeled samples, 15 engineered features, and a classification task with 8 classes. Which model family is most appropriate?

options.C: Random Forest or Support Vector Machine

questions.metadata: Low-data regime model selection. RF and SVM are well-suited for small, structured feature sets.

questions.question: You wrap a signal cable in copper foil tape for EMI shielding. To be effective while avoiding a ground loop, the foil should be connected to ground:

options.A: At the signal source end only
options.D: At the midpoint of the cable

questions.metadata: Shield grounding — single-end grounding prevents ground loops while still providing shielding. Classic analog layout rule.

questions.question: A part is designed to be exactly 50.0 mm wide. Your caliper reads 50.4 mm. What is the dimensional error as a percentage?
questions.metadata: Basic dimensional maths: 0.4 / 50 × 100 = 0.8%. Tests whether candidate actually uses a caliper and thinks in tolerances.

questions.question: A piezo disc actuator is most commonly damaged by:

options.D: Excessive drive voltage or rapid heat buildup

questions.metadata: Practical safety knowledge. Over-voltage and thermal shock are the main piezo failure modes.

questions.question: A teammate commits 200 MB raw data files directly into a Git repository. The correct practice is:

options.A: This is fine — Git can handle large files
options.B: Use Git LFS or store data in a separate object store with only metadata in the repo
options.D: Delete the repository and start again

questions.metadata: Git hygiene for data-heavy projects. Git LFS or separate storage is the standard approach.

questions.question: You plan to run 60 measurements on the same physical object over two weeks. Why is it critical to mark and log its orientation before every run?

options.A: For aesthetic consistency in documentation photos
options.B: To comply with laboratory safety regulations
options.C: To track weight distribution changes over time
options.D: Inconsistent orientation changes which resonance modes are excited, breaking repeatability

questions.metadata: Hidden failure mode — orientation affects mode shape excitation. Only candidates who have done real modal measurement know this intuitively.

questions.question: Your measurement results drift by 3% over a 2-hour session, with no code changes. The correct first diagnostic step is:

options.B: Check environmental conditions — temperature and humidity affect material properties and sensor behaviour
options.C: Rewrite the capture software from scratch

questions.metadata: Systematic debugging instinct. Environmental drift is the most common cause of session-to-session variation in physical measurements.
